title: Investing 101 with a longtime broker — Mar 14 2019 conversation
html:
39-minute Q&A between teenage Yahir and a 41-year-veteran financial broker covering blue-chip vs small-cap funds, market timing, dollar-cost averaging, shorting, and gold-fund moves during the 2007–08 crash. Recorded in grants pass or during grants pass teen era; the broker repeatedly cites john mauldin economics newsletters and Stansberry / Motley Fool / Kiplinger as his information edge — same reading list Yahir later names in his Jan 2020 time capsule recording.

TL;DR

  • A Grants Pass broker walks 15-year-old Yahir through small-cap → mid-cap → blue-chip growth pyramid, the "rising tide raises all ships" frame, and how panic-selling in short term downturns is what kills retail investors.
  • Broker brags about moving 8 clients out of mutual funds into gold during the pre 2008 crash window after john mauldin flagged it — 240% return over 28 months — Yahir takes the mental note.
  • Closing Q&A: best advice to your 18-year-old self → "started investing from the get-go, didn't wait until late 30s" — pairs with Yahir's later regret minimization framework thinking and the financial freedom thread.

john mauldin · grants pass or · financial freedom · long term vs short term thinking · regret minimization framework · value investing

related.label: Time-capsule recording — Jan 2 2020 'recording with my life'
